name: Pedalboard (Spotify's Python Audio Effects Library)

performance.notes: C++ JUCE backend; faster than pure Python; streaming processing supported

agent_readiness.notes: Spotify's professional-grade audio effects for Python via JUCE backend. from pedalboard import Pedalboard, Chorus, Reverb, Compressor, HighShelfFilter, NoiseGate. from pedalboard.io import AudioFile. board = Pedalboard([Chorus(), Reverb(room_size=0.25)]). with AudioFile('input.wav') as f: audio = f.read(f.frames). with AudioFile('output.wav', 'w', 44100, 2) as f: f.write(board(audio, sample_rate=44100)). Streaming: process in chunks. VST3/AU plugin loading (macOS). MP3 I/O. Noise reduction. Pitch shifting. Used for ML audio data augmentation.

gotchas: VST3/AU plugin loading is macOS/Windows only
Limited to audio effects — not a general audio analysis library
JUCE dependency makes wheel sizes large
best_when: Audio data augmentation for ML training (reverb, noise, pitch shifts)
Batch audio processing with professional-quality effects
When pydub's ffmpeg-based processing lacks quality or speed
avoid_when: Audio feature extraction for ML (use librosa)
Real-time streaming processing (latency may be too high)
summary: Pedalboard is Spotify's Python audio effects library via C++ JUCE backend. Pedalboard([Chorus(), Reverb(), Compressor()])(audio, sample_rate=44100). AudioFile for MP3/WAV I/O. Streaming chunk processing. VST3/AU plugin loading (macOS). Faster and higher quality than pydub for effects. Used for ML audio data augmentation (reverb, noise, pitch). GPL-3.0. Best for professional-quality audio effects and batch processing.
